findings.issue: The example remote-frontend configuration still hard-codes VITE_APP_BASE_URL to http://localhost:3000, and the OAuth entrypoints prefer that env var over window.location.origin when constructing return_to/callback URLs. A user following the checked-in example and opening the app from http://<lan-ip>:3002 will send OAuth back to localhost:3000 instead of the actual LAN origin, losing the sessionStorage PKCE verifier and breaking exactly the non-loopback flow this workstream is meant to fix.
findings.remediation: Remove VITE_APP_BASE_URL from the example or set/document it as the actual frontend origin; prefer leaving it unset so OAuth callbacks use window.location.origin for LAN access.

findings.issue: The OAuth browser regression suite is currently blocked by an unrelated frontend Vitest gate in Playwright globalSetup. Running `npx playwright test auth.spec.ts` in remote-frontend never reaches the auth spec because global setup executes the entire sibling frontend test suite and exits on its unrelated failures, so this workstream's browser-level OAuth verification is coupled to unrelated UI test churn.
findings.remediation: Move the frontend SC4 guard out of Playwright globalSetup or make it a separate CI/preflight command so remote-frontend OAuth E2E tests can run and report their own failures independently.
